channel.title: Forest SD Blog
channel.description: Thoughts on technology, SEO, digital marketing, and life as a digital native in San Diego.

item.title: Google Made AI Image Generation a Profile Product
item.author: forestsd2026@gmail.com (Forest SD)
item.description: Google's June 29 personalized Gemini rollout and June 30 Nano Banana rollout show the next consumer AI moat isn't prompt tricks. It's profile-based generation built on your photos, taste, and context.

item.title: Cloudflare Just Gave AI Agents Burner Accounts
item.author: forestsd2026@gmail.com (Forest SD)
item.description: Cloudflare's June 19 temporary-account rollout is a preview of a different web, where software gets short-lived identity and limited authority before a human ever logs in.

item.title: OpenAI Wants to Be the Patch Layer for the Internet
item.author: forestsd2026@gmail.com (Forest SD)
item.description: OpenAI's June 22 Patch the Planet rollout shows where AI security is heading next: away from bug-count theater and toward owning the patch workflow for shared software infrastructure.

item.title: The Real Creative AI Product Is Taste Memory
item.author: forestsd2026@gmail.com (Forest SD)
item.description: Adobe's June 18 Firefly update shows where creative AI is heading next: away from one-off generation and toward tools that remember your style, assets, and decisions.

item.title: There Is Now One Web for People and Another for Agents
item.author: forestsd2026@gmail.com (Forest SD)
item.description: Microsoft's Web IQ and Google's AI Mode numbers show the web splitting into a browsing layer for people and a grounding layer for AI agents.

item.title: AI Safety Just Turned Into Export-Control Theater
item.author: forestsd2026@gmail.com (Forest SD)
item.description: Anthropic's June 12 Fable 5 shutdown shows frontier AI is moving from safety PDFs to export controls, access restrictions, and hard state power.

item.title: Apple Finally Turned AI Into a Default Setting
item.author: forestsd2026@gmail.com (Forest SD)
item.description: WWDC26 did not prove Apple invented the future of AI. It proved something more important: when Apple finally ships, AI stops being a demo and starts becoming a default.

item.title: Meta Did Not Automate Support. It Automated Account Takeover.
item.author: forestsd2026@gmail.com (Forest SD)
item.description: Meta's AI support bot let attackers walk through Instagram account recovery by chat. That is not an AI glitch. It is a permissions failure dressed up as automation.

item.title: Bots Passed Humans Online. The Web Was Not Built for This.
item.author: forestsd2026@gmail.com (Forest SD)
item.description: Cloudflare says bots just passed humans online. That is not a trivia stat. It means the web's old traffic, ad, and trust model is starting to crack.

item.title: The UK Just Forced Google to Admit AI Search Needs Publishers
item.author: forestsd2026@gmail.com (Forest SD)
item.description: On June 3, 2026, the UK forced Google to offer AI-search opt-outs, clearer attribution, and publisher metrics. That is not generosity. It is an admission that AI search still depends on the open web.

item.title: Typing Into Apps Is Starting to Look Old
item.author: forestsd2026@gmail.com (Forest SD)
item.description: Google's Gmail Live, Docs voice prompts, and Gemini Spark all point to the same shift: software is moving away from menus and search boxes and toward conversational intent layers.

item.title: OpenAI's Math Breakthrough Is Not Really About Math
item.author: forestsd2026@gmail.com (Forest SD)
item.description: OpenAI says a general-purpose reasoning model disproved a central conjecture in discrete geometry. The real shift is not math. It is what that means for elite knowledge work.

item.title: Google Wants Search to Be Your Operating System
item.author: forestsd2026@gmail.com (Forest SD)
item.description: At Google I/O 2026, Google stopped pitching Search as a results page and started pitching it as an ambient agent layer wired into your tabs, inbox, shopping, and glasses.

item.title: Google's AI Search Needs the Open Web More Than It Wants to Admit
item.author: forestsd2026@gmail.com (Forest SD)
item.description: Google is adding more links and subscription hooks to AI Overviews because AI search cannot survive if it keeps starving the publishers and websites it depends on.

item.title: Digg's AI Comeback Thinks the Internet Still Lives on X
item.author: forestsd2026@gmail.com (Forest SD)
item.description: Digg's latest reboot ranks AI news by watching X engagement, sentiment, and influencer graphs. That is not the return of web discovery. It's nostalgia wrapped around a brittle dependency.

item.title: Any Lawful Use Is Not an AI Safety Policy
item.author: forestsd2026@gmail.com (Forest SD)
item.description: Google's reported Pentagon AI deal says its models can be used for 'any lawful government purpose.' That phrase should worry anyone pretending legality is the same thing as restraint.

item.title: The Most Important AI Release This Week Was a Postmortem
item.author: forestsd2026@gmail.com (Forest SD)
item.description: OpenAI launched GPT-5.5. Anthropic published a blunt postmortem explaining why Claude got worse. The postmortem matters more, because trust beats benchmark theater.

item.title: Apple Just Picked Hardware Over AI Hype
item.author: forestsd2026@gmail.com (Forest SD)
item.description: Tim Cook moving to executive chairman and John Ternus taking over as CEO is not routine succession news. Apple just signaled that hardware still matters more than AI theater.

item.title: Claude Design Means the Mockup Layer Is Officially Cheap
item.author: forestsd2026@gmail.com (Forest SD)
item.description: Anthropic's Claude Design turns prototypes, decks, and marketing mockups into promptable output. The real story is not prettier drafts. It's that draft-layer design just got cheap.

item.title: Someone Turned 31 WordPress Plugins Into a Google Poisoning Machine
item.author: forestsd2026@gmail.com (Forest SD)
item.description: A six-figure WordPress plugin portfolio got sold, backdoored, and used to inject hidden SEO spam. This wasn't a one-off bug. It's the plugin trust model breaking in public.

item.title: Topical AEO and SEO in 2026: What Actually Wins Now
item.author: forestsd2026@gmail.com (Forest SD)
item.description: A practical guide to topical AEO and SEO in 2026, including AI Overviews, entity signals, topical authority, structured content, and what marketers should do next.

item.title: Meta Spent $135 Billion to Play Catch-Up — And Muse Spark Is What They Got
item.author: forestsd2026@gmail.com (Forest SD)
item.description: Meta's new Muse Spark model is the first output from their $14.3B Superintelligence Lab. It's small, it's fast, and it still can't code as well as the competition. Here's what that tells us.

item.title: AI Is Writing Code 10x Faster Than Anyone Can Review It
item.author: forestsd2026@gmail.com (Forest SD)
item.description: Companies went from 25,000 to 250,000 lines of code per month overnight. Nobody can review it. Nobody can secure it. The code overload crisis is here.

item.title: Anthropic's Most Powerful Model Got Leaked Before They Could Launch It
item.author: forestsd2026@gmail.com (Forest SD)
item.description: Anthropic's secret 'Mythos' model — a step change in AI capabilities — was accidentally exposed in a public data cache. It's also apparently a cybersecurity nightmare. Here's what that means.

item.title: OpenAI Is Buying Everything and Merging It Into One App
item.author: forestsd2026@gmail.com (Forest SD)
item.description: OpenAI acquired Astral, merged ChatGPT + Codex + a browser into a superapp, and is now gunning for Anthropic. Six acquisitions in 2026 already. This is a consolidation play, not a product play.

item.title: Perplexity's "Always-On" AI Is a Different Kind of Product
item.author: forestsd2026@gmail.com (Forest SD)
item.description: Perplexity didn't launch a chatbot. They launched something that stays on, remembers your stuff, and keeps working while you sleep. That's a fundamentally different bet.

item.title: Britannica Is Suing OpenAI for Stealing the Encyclopedia
item.author: forestsd2026@gmail.com (Forest SD)
item.description: Encyclopedia Britannica is suing OpenAI for scraping 100,000 articles, running them through RAG, and then hallucinating their name on the outputs. This one hits different.

item.title: Nvidia Just Made AI Agents Mainstream — And Nobody's Ready
item.author: forestsd2026@gmail.com (Forest SD)
item.description: Jensen Huang spent 3 hours at GTC 2026 making one thing clear: the era of AI agents is here. NemoClaw, $1T in chip orders, a Disney robot. What it actually means.

item.title: The AI Layoffs Are Real Now
item.author: forestsd2026@gmail.com (Forest SD)
item.description: 45,000 tech workers laid off in early 2026. 9,200 directly attributed to AI. Block just cut 40% of its staff. This isn't a warning anymore — it's happening.

item.title: I Automated My Entire Workflow. Here's What Actually Happened.
item.author: forestsd2026@gmail.com (Forest SD)
item.description: What happens when you automate everything you can? I built scripts for 270+ sites, email, social media, analytics, and backups. Here's what I learned.

item.title: When an AI Company Says No to the Pentagon
item.author: forestsd2026@gmail.com (Forest SD)
item.description: Anthropic got blacklisted by the Pentagon for refusing to let their AI be used for autonomous weapons. What this means for every other AI company.

item.title: AI Search Is Full of Dark Patterns and Nobody's Talking About It
item.author: forestsd2026@gmail.com (Forest SD)
item.description: AI-powered search is reshaping how we find information. But the incentive structures are creating new dark patterns that make the old SEO spam look quaint.

item.title: The State of Web Development in 2026: What's Changed
item.author: forestsd2026@gmail.com (Forest SD)
item.description: Static sites are back, AI writes code now, and performance is everything. A developer's honest take on web development in 2026 — what's changed, what hasn't, and what matters.

item.title: Why San Diego's Tech Scene Is Quietly Exploding
item.author: forestsd2026@gmail.com (Forest SD)
item.description: San Diego's tech ecosystem is booming — biotech, defense tech, AI startups, and a digital scene that rivals bigger cities. A local perspective on why SD is the place to watch.

item.title: The State of Local SEO in 2026: What Actually Works
item.author: forestsd2026@gmail.com (Forest SD)
item.description: Practical, no-BS guide to local SEO in 2026. Google AI Overviews, entity optimization, review signals, GBP strategies, and schema markup that actually moves the needle.

item.title: Entity SEO: The Complete Guide for 2026
item.author: forestsd2026@gmail.com (Forest SD)
item.description: Everything you need to know about entity SEO in 2026. Knowledge Graph optimization, JSON-LD structured data, entity-query overlap, and practical steps for local businesses.

item.title: How Digital Natives Are Redefining Creativity in the AI Era
item.author: forestsd2026@gmail.com (Forest SD)
item.description: A digital native's perspective on how AI tools are reshaping creative workflows, blending human intuition with machine capability across content creation, design, and marketing.

item.title: Content Strategy for Small Businesses That Actually Drives Revenue
item.author: forestsd2026@gmail.com (Forest SD)
item.description: Why most small business blogs fail and what to do instead. Hub-and-spoke content, topical authority, local content strategies, and how to measure content ROI beyond traffic.

item.title: AEO: How to Optimize for AI Search Engines
item.author: forestsd2026@gmail.com (Forest SD)
item.description: AI Engine Optimization explained. How to get cited by ChatGPT, Perplexity, and Google AI Overviews. Content structure, source selection, and actionable tactics for 2026.
